channel.title: AI Tech Daily
channel.description: AI Tech Daily is your free daily AI news podcast â€” 5 minutes of the biggest artificial intelligence and tech news every morning. Stay ahead of AI, machine learning, ChatGPT, OpenAI, Google, and the tech industry. New episode every day, completely free.
channel.itunes:author: AI Tech Daily

item.title: AI News Jun 29 — Prompt injection is exploiting enterprise AI'
item.description: The AI revolution is in full swing, but a dark cloud is looming over the enterprise AI landscape - hackers have discovered a way to exploit the biggest design flaws in large language models, and it's got everyone on high alert. In the past two years, businesses have been rushing to integrate AI into every aspect of their operations, from support and analytics to development and automation. But as it turns out, this rapid adoption has also created a slew of new vulnerabilities that cybercriminals...

item.title: AI News Jun 29 — Prompt injection is exploiting enterprise AI'
item.description: Imagine a world where the most powerful AI systems are being exploited by cybercriminals, targeting the very heart of enterprise AI - that's the reality we're facing today. In the past two years, businesses have been rapidly adopting large language models, or LLMs, to fit into support, analytics, development, and internal automation, but along with this adoption, a new trend has emerged: cybercriminals are taking advantage of the disconnect between assumptions about LLMs and their actual charact...

item.title: AI News Jun 30 — Meituan open sources LongCat-2.0, the 1.6T, n
item.description: Imagine a world where AI models are no longer controlled by a select few, but are free for anyone to use and improve. That world just got a whole lot closer, thanks to Chinese delivery app company Meituan, which has just open-sourced its LongCat-2.0 model. This 1.6T near-frontier agentic coding model has been leading the way on OpenRouter, and the fact that it was trained entirely on Chinese chips is a significant milestone. It shows that countries like China are making strides in AI development...

item.title: AI Tech Daily | Jun 30 — Meituan open sources LongCat-2.0, the 1.6T, near-f
item.description: Imagine a world where artificial intelligence is no longer controlled by a select few, but is open to everyone, and a major player in the Chinese tech scene just made a huge step towards making that a reality. Meituan, the company behind the popular Chinese delivery app, has just open-sourced its LongCat-2.0 model, a 1.6 trillion parameter agentic coding model that's been leading the OpenRouter platform. This is a massive deal, not just because of the model's impressive capabilities, but also be...

item.title: AI Tech Daily | Jun 30 — Meituan open sources LongCat-2.0, the 1.6T, near-f
item.description: Imagine a world where coding is no longer a tedious task, but rather a collaborative effort between humans and machines, and a major step towards that reality just happened with Meituan's open-sourcing of LongCat-2.0, a 1.6 trillion parameter agentic coding model that's been leading the charge on OpenRouter. What's significant about LongCat-2.0 is that it was trained entirely on Chinese chips, marking a significant milestone in the country's quest for technological independence. This matters bec...

item.title: AI Tech Daily | Jul 01 — Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 at a steep disc
item.description: Imagine having access to cutting-edge AI technology without breaking the bank - that's exactly what Anthropic is offering with the launch of Claude Sonnet 5, a new AI model that delivers near-flagship performance at mid-tier prices. This move is a strategic one, as the company gears up for a highly anticipated initial public offering that will put its valuation to the test. By offering a more affordable option, Anthropic is making powerful agentic capabilities accessible to cost-conscious enterp...

item.title: AI Tech Daily | Jul 02 — Anthropic is bringing back Claude Fable 5 globally
item.description: In a stunning turnaround, Anthropic is bringing back its powerful AI model, Claude Fable 5, globally, after the US Department of Commerce lifted its export control order, and this move is going to be a game-changer for enterprises around the world. Just a few weeks ago, Anthropic was forced to suspend all global access to Fable 5 due to the export control order, which was issued on June 12, 2026. But now, with the order withdrawn, Anthropic is restoring access to this highly advanced AI model, a...

item.title: AI Tech Daily | Jul 03 — Enterprises lost Claude Fable 5 for a few weeks. N
item.description: Imagine waking up one morning to find that the most powerful AI model you rely on is suddenly gone, with no warning and no timeline for its return - that's exactly what happened to enterprises that use Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 model just a few weeks ago. The model, which is considered the most capable on the market, was taken offline for every customer due to a US export-control order, leaving many businesses scrambling to find alternative solutions. However, new data shows that two-thirds of ...

item.title: AI Tech Daily | Jul 04 — Anthropic is bringing back Claude Fable 5 globally
item.description: In a stunning reversal, the US Department of Commerce has withdrawn its emergency export controls on Anthropic's Claude Fable 5, the most powerful generally released AI model yet, and the company is wasting no time in restoring global access. This move comes just weeks after the export control order was issued, forcing Anthropic to suspend all global access to Fable 5, leaving enterprises scrambling to find alternative solutions. The impact was significant, with many companies relying on Fable 5...

item.title: AI Tech Daily | Jul 05 — Anthropic is bringing back Claude Fable 5 globally
item.description: Get ready for a major boost in AI capabilities, as Anthropic is reviving global access to its powerhouse model, Claude Fable 5, after the US lifted its export control order. This is huge news, especially for enterprises that were left hanging when the order first came into effect on June 12. But now, with the restrictions lifted, companies worldwide can once again tap into the immense potential of Claude Fable 5. So, where can they access it? The good news is that Anthropic is restoring global a...

item.title: Anthropic is bringing back Claude Fable 5 globally after US — AI Tech Daily | Jul 06
item.description: Two-thirds of enterprises had already built a hedge against AI model uncertainty before the US government's sudden export control order took down Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 model, and it turns out that caution was well-founded. The order, which was issued on June 12, 2026, led Anthropic to suspend all global access to Fable 5, leaving many businesses scrambling to adjust their AI strategies. But now, after the US Department of Commerce withdrew the emergency export controls, Anthropic is restori...

item.title: Hot French startup ZML releases free product to speed — AI Tech Daily | Jul 08
item.description: $1 billion is what AI chip maker SambaNova just raised at an $11 billion valuation, a staggering five months after its last mega funding round. This massive investment not only showcases the tremendous growth potential of the AI chip market but also highlights SambaNova's position as a leader in this space. The fact that SambaNova was rumored to be in talks with Intel for a potential acquisition at a much lower valuation just a few months ago makes this fundraising round even more impressive. Th...

item.title: SpaceX's Grok 4.5 launches at half the price of rivals — AI Tech Daily | Jul 09
item.description: Elon Musk's SpaceX just launched Grok 4.5, an artificial intelligence model that can be had for half the price of its rivals, and this could send shockwaves through the AI community, particularly at Anthropic and OpenAI. Grok 4.5 is the first tangible product of SpaceX's $60 billion acquisition of AI coding startup Cursor, completed just weeks ago, and it's specifically designed for coding and autonomous agents. The launch marks a major test of the sprawling AI empire Musk has assembled over the...

item.title: Enterprises using multiple AI models are underestimating — AI Tech Daily | Jul 10
item.description: Enterprises using multiple AI models are underestimating failure rates by 2.25x, a shocking statistic that has major implications for the future of AI safety. This staggering number comes from a new study evaluating 67 frontier models from 21 providers, which reveals that the assumption that combining multiple models will cover each other's blind spots is mathematically flawed. The concept, known as the co-failure ceiling, shows that the real limit on orchestration is not how often models disagr...
